title: Declare-once codegen: generate the activity plumbing from the typed declaration
purpose: The keystone of the authoring cluster (RM-021). Today an activity is hand-mirrored across five to seven files that must agree byte-for-byte; this brief makes the typed activity declaration the single source of truth (ADR-014) and generates the rest. It extends the existing aion codegen machinery (which already turns I/O schemas into codecs, ADR-007 precedent) from workflow I/O to the full activity surface, with a drift gate so generated output can never silently rot.
task: Make the typed activity declaration the only per-activity artifact an author writes, and generate the plumbing from it. In: an activity declaration form (a typed signature plus a tier annotation InVm | RemotePython | RemoteRust) in the aion_flow activities surface; an `aion generate` command that derives the activity.new wrapper, the value-type codec pairs, the worker handler stub for the tier, the worker registration entry, the workflow.toml activities entry, and the wire-compat golden; an `aion generate --check` drift gate; a round-trip guarantee (delete all generated files, regenerate byte-identical); and an authoring guide. Out: the dev loop (WA-002), server-side compile (WA-003), the determinism linter and test/input scaffolds (WA-007), and generating the side-effecting bodies of activities (the author writes those; the determinism boundary is unchanged).

requirements.title: Typed activity declaration form with a tier annotation
requirements.spec: WHEN an author declares an activity, THE SYSTEM SHALL accept a typed signature plus a tier annotation (InVm | RemotePython | RemoteRust) in the activities surface that the generator reads to drive codegen. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT require the author to also hand-write the activity.new wrapper, the codecs, the worker handler, the registration entry, or the manifest entry for that activity.
acceptance: An activity declaration form exists in gleam/aion_flow/src/aion/activity.gleam that carries the activity's input type, output type, and tier.
A declared activity type-checks under gleam build with no hand-written activity.new wrapper present.
The declaration is the only per-activity artifact the author writes — verified by an example activity whose wrapper/codecs/worker/registration/manifest are all absent from hand-written source.

requirements.title: aion generate derives the activity plumbing
requirements.spec: WHEN `aion generate` runs against a package, THE SYSTEM SHALL derive from each typed declaration the activity.new wrapper, the value-type codec pairs, the worker handler stub for the declared tier, the worker registration entry, the workflow.toml activities entry, and the wire-compat golden. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT emit any retry policy, timeout, or backoff value the author did not declare (ADR-001).
acceptance: Running `aion generate` on examples/order-saga emits the activity.new wrappers, codec pairs, worker handler stubs, registration entries, and workflow.toml activities entries for every declared activity.
Each remote activity gets a wire-compat golden derived from its value-type codecs, with no hand-derived literal.

No generated activity configuration contains a retry/timeout/backoff value absent from the declaration — verified by generating an activity with no policy and asserting the generated config carries none.

requirements.title: Drift gate, round-trip guarantee, and the authoring guide
requirements.spec: WHEN `aion generate --check` runs, THE SYSTEM SHALL regenerate every generated file in memory and exit non-zero if any on-disk generated file differs. WHEN every generated file for a package is deleted and `aion generate` is re-run, THE SYSTEM SHALL reproduce them byte-identical.
acceptance: `aion generate --check` exits non-zero on a hand-edited generated file and exits zero when the tree is clean.
Deleting every generated file for examples/order-saga and running `aion generate` reproduces them byte-identical, proven by a round-trip test.
docs/authoring.md walks the declare -> generate -> run model end to end using the saga.

boundaries: SHALL NOT generate the side-effecting body of any activity or workflow — only the plumbing that must agree byte-for-byte.
SHALL NOT build the dev loop (WA-002), the server compile loop (WA-003), or the determinism/test/input scaffolds (WA-007).
SHALL NOT introduce any default retry policy, timeout, or backoff (ADR-001).
verification: clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings and cargo fmt --check pass clean; gleam format --check clean for the SDK change.
The delete-all-and-regenerate round-trip is byte-identical for examples/order-saga.
The --check drift gate is proven in both directions (clean tree passes, hand-edit fails).
